rag_rules: AGREE annex blocks must be placed in component implementation scopes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments.
Implementation-level contracts should only reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables in assign statements.
generic_rules: Assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports must be placed in valid component type scopes.
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
Generated AGREE code must not introduce package-qualified identifiers not present in the analyzed model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assign statements in implementation scope must reference existing output features or declared variables.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use type scope for output dependency contracts.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers not found in the AADL model.
